title: MinijailProfile
description: Typed minijail profile metadata referenced by process roles.

adr_carve_out.description: ADR or plan carve-out reference for uid/root-capable exceptions.

capabilities.description: Linux capabilities retained by the jailed process.

cgroupPlacement.description: Cgroup placement declaration for broker delegation.

exceptionRef.description: Optional ADR or plan reference justifying privileged exceptions.

gid.description: Numeric gid used after minijail drops privilege.

mountPolicy.description: Mount policy metadata, including writable paths.

namespaces.description: Namespace isolation requested for this role.

profileId.description: Stable profile identifier used from processes.json.

requiresStartRoot.description: Whether this long-lived profile may start as root under an ADR-listed exception.

seccompPolicyRef.description: Seccomp policy reference; kernel-version syscall allowlists are out of scope.

uid.description: Numeric uid used after minijail drops privilege.

umask.description: v1.1.2fu36: file-creation mask the broker installs in the spawned child before execve. Lets profiles that bind shared sockets (vhost-user-sound, crosvm-gpu, crosvm video, swtpm) declare an umask of `0o007` so the resulting sockets have group r-w (mode 0660/0770) and the existing default ACL on the per-VM runtime dir yields effective r-w for named-user entries (cloud-hypervisor uid). Without this, the default 0o022 umask yields socket mode 0644 — but vhost-user/swtpm bind() typically restricts to 0600 — which derives ACL mask=0 and named-user entries become ineffective. None means "inherit the broker's umask" (current behaviour).

userNamespace.description: v1.1.1fu14 (ADR 0021): when `Some`, the broker pre-establishes a single-entry user namespace for runners using this profile. The child is fake-root inside the namespace (all caps within the user-NS scope) and the host-side `capabilities` set should typically be empty. Currently consumed by virtiofsd roles for least-privilege FS serving without CAP_DAC_*. Profiles that don't need a user NS omit this field.

BindMount.description: Cross-domain bind mount declaration for a minijail role.

dst.description: Destination path inside the role's mount namespace.

src.description: Source path on the host (broker opens on the caller's behalf).

CgroupPlacement.description: Cgroup placement for a jailed role.

delegated.description: Whether the broker must delegate ownership to nixlingd.

subtree.description: cgroup-v2 subtree requested for this role.

MountPolicy.description: Mount policy for a minijail role.

bindMounts.description: v1.1.1 live-deploy fu9: cross-domain bind mounts the broker performs into the role's mount namespace (e.g. a Wayland socket for the GPU sidecar). Each entry is a `{src, dst}` pair. Optional for backward-compat with older bundles.

deviceBinds.description: v1.1.1 live-deploy fu9: closed-set device bind paths the broker opens on behalf of the role (e.g. `/dev/kvm`, `/dev/dri/renderD128`, `/dev/nvidia*`). Optional for backward-compat with older bundles; empty for roles that don't need device access.

hideDeviceNodesByDefault.description: Whether device nodes are hidden unless broker-opened fds are passed.

readOnlyPaths.description: Read-only paths mounted into the jail.

writablePaths.description: Writable paths explicitly documented for this role.

NamespaceSet.description: Namespace flags for a minijail profile.

ipc.description: Whether the IPC namespace is isolated.

mount.description: Whether the mount namespace is isolated.

net.description: Whether the network namespace is isolated.

pid.description: Whether the PID namespace is isolated.

user.description: Whether a user namespace is used.

uts.description: Whether the UTS namespace is isolated.

UserNamespaceProfile.description: v1.1.1fu14 — single-entry uid_map/gid_map declaration for the per-role user namespace. The values are the host UIDs/GIDs that in-NS UID 0 (and GID 0) map to. See ADR 0021.

WritablePath.description: Writable path declaration with a purpose string.

path.description: Absolute path made writable in the jail.
